कमाईबाट लगानी गर्नुपर्छ, त्यसबाट रोजगारी र थप आम्दानी सिर्जना हुन्छ तर त्यसको लागि Financial Literacy चाहिन्छ। मैले के मा कति रकम, कति समय लगानी गर्नुपर्छ? के गर्नु, कसरी जीउनु, के खानु ठीक भन्ने निर्णय लिन सक्नुपर्छ अहिलेको खाडल भनेकै त्यही हो प्राय नेपालीको। डायस्पोराले सीप र लगानी नेपाल भित्राउन पनि नेपालको भ्रष्टाचार र अब्यवस्था बाधक छ यसलाई पहिले हटाउन नेपालमा भएका मानिसको सोच परिवर्तन हुनु आवश्यक छ।

As someone who stayed abroad for almost a decade and currently in Nepal, my view points on why Youths are leaving (and will continue to leave):
1) Pressure from family and society. Aruko chora chori le esto garyo testo kinyo etc. People here feel discouraged.
2) To much talk only about politics jata gaye pani. Blaming Netas for everything while not realizing how corrupt we have become. Corruption in rampant in this country.
3) Blame game, j ni arulai dos layo.
4) Country cant just survive on IT sector ko ali ali jobs, we need to create niche exports for long-term to create jobs outside IT.
5) People need to stop being individualistic and work for greater good.

6) Solid Development roadmaps.

I am still bullish on Nepal and will continue to work here till its possible to sustain livelihood.

If I take a step back, post-World War 2, both Germany and Japan's youth and the entire nation worked hard to rebuild their nation. Some such sense needs to be imbibed right at the school stage in Nepal so as to bring nation-building pride and economic contentment. It is not a very long haul, but rightly done, we can perhaps see positive results in just over a decade. Just blaming the government is an excuse. But sadly to majority of Nepali youths this sounds like poetry. Social media and peer pressure is spoiling our young minds right at the school level leaving an already jeopardized nation to darker abyss. Our boys and girls want to settle the rest of their lives in the best nations (Mostly just following the trend and FOMO). Economic reason is not universal in all migrations.

BUT BEWARE. REVERSE MIGRATION is happening. The developed host nations like Canada, US, Australia, Japan are already getting overwhelmed with migrants from poor countries. Lack of housing, rising food prices and electricity bills, racism, safety, long process for healthcare facilities and lack of jobs are just the struggles that has to be kept in mind. People from some poor countries are returning back to their nations citing above mentioned reasons. That is going to increase more. ANTI IMMIGRATION SENTIMENTS IS ON THE RISE.
